title: Arthur Harrow
content:
{{Otheruses|Moon Knight}} {{CharHub|Arthur Harrow|Arthur Harrow}} {{Character |image = Arthur Harrow Promo.png |real name = Arthur Harrow{{Ref|MK101CC}}{{ref|MK101}} |alias = {{Alias |codenames = Moon Knight{{Ref|MK102}} |nicknames = Fist of Vengeance{{Ref|MK102}}}} |species = [[Human]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|USA}} |gender = Male |DOD = [[2025|May 2025]]{{Ref|MK101}}{{Ref|MSTMCUAOT}} |affiliation = [[Disciples of Ammit]] |tv series = ''[[Moon Knight (TV series)|Moon Knight]]'' (5 episodes) |actor = [[Ethan Hawke]] |status = Deceased}} {{Quote|[[Ammit|She]]'s out there, waiting, longing to be freed. While the cruel masses deserve to face her judgment. And in the wake of their screams, evil eradicated.|Arthur Harrow to [[Steven Grant]]|Summon the Suit}} '''Arthur Harrow''' was a former Avatar of [[Khonshu]] and a previous wielder of the '''Moon Knight''' title, who fell disillusioned with Khonshu and instead became devoted to [[Ammit]]. He formed a [[Disciples of Ammit|cult]] of disciples dedicated to bringing her back to life and bestowing her judgment on the world. Harrow's mission to locate her [[Tomb of Alexander the Great|tomb]] and resurrect her was hindered by the present Moon Knight, [[Moon Knight|Marc Spector]], who he was forced to kill. Harrow succeeded in liberating Ammit from her imprisonment and became her Avatar, but he was stopped by a resurrected Moon Knight and [[Scarlet Scarab]], who imprisoned Ammit inside his body. Harrow was administrated to a [[Sienkiewicz Psychiatric Hospital|psychiatric hospital]], where he was later assassinated by Spector's alter [[Jake Lockley]], killing Ammit as well. ==Biography== ===Early Life=== ====Avatar for Khonshu==== {{Quote|Can I tell you a secret? I enjoyed dealing out pain on your behalf. That is the greatest sin I carry.|Arthur Harrow to [[Khonshu]]|The Friendly Type}} Arthur Harrow was chosen to be [[Khonshu]]'s Avatar and operated as "Moon Knight". However, due to Khonshu's constant need for him to perform tasks, thus never letting him be free, Harrow lost faith in him and was eventually persuaded by [[Ammit]] to betray Khonshu and swear allegiance to her.{{Ref|MK102}} ===Cult Leader=== ====Judgement in the Alps==== [[File:Arthur Harrow & his followers.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow being worshipped by his [[Disciples of Ammit|followers]]]] {{Quote|I've been good my entire life."
"I believe you. But the [[Scales of Justice|scales]] see everything. Perhaps it's something that lies ahead. Well... I wish you could live to see the world we make. Yet, [[Ammit]] has decided.|[[List of Minor Characters/Phase Four#The Goldfish Problem|Alpine Woman]] and Arthur Harrow|The Goldfish Problem}} Harrow took up residence in a penthouse situated in a village in the [[Austria|Alps]] in [[Austria]], amassed a following of [[Disciples of Ammit|disciples]], crushed glass into his shoes before putting them on each morning, and using the power of [[Ammit]] that was given to him in a [[Staff of Ammit|cane]] to judge people's morality and kill people whom his ability deemed unworthy. [[File:Call me Arthur.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow asks for people to come forward]] At one judgment ceremony, a man approached Harrow to be judged, and Harrow placed his cane in the man's hands. Harrow watched his tattoo turn green and announced to the public that the man was a good person. [[File:Harrow with old woman.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow judging a [[List of Minor Characters/Phase Four#The Goldfish Problem|woman]]'s fate]] An [[List of Minor Characters/Phase Four#The Goldfish Problem|elderly woman]] then approached Harrow and asked to be judged, so Harrow repeated the process for her. Harrow's tattoo turned red, despite the woman's insistence that she had been good her entire life. Harrow said that while he believed her, his tattoo was never wrong. He suggested that the woman was destined to do something bad in the future and expressed sorrow that woman would not see Harrow's future before using his power to kill her. [[File:Arthur Harrow meets Steven Grant.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow first encountering [[Steven Grant]]]] While the woman's body was being carried away, two of Harrow's disciples told him that someone had [[Theft of the Scarab of Ammit|stolen]] the [[Scarab of Ammit]], and that the thief was likely at the ceremony. Harrow chanted in ancient [[Egypt|Egyptian]] for the group to kneel, and everyone did so except [[Steven Grant]], who hesitated before following suit. Harrow recognized Grant as a mercenary named [[Marc Spector]] and demanded that the scarab be returned to him. [[File:Arthur Harrow and Steven Grant.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow demanding the return of the [[Scarab of Ammit|scarab]]]] Grant attempted to give Harrow the scarab, but could not release the grip he had. Grant began walking away and insisting that he was not responsible for his actions, so Harrow had his disciples grab Grant and retrieve the scarab. However, Harrow watched as Grant changed into Spector and killed the men grabbing him, before Grant resumed control and ran away hopping into a cupcake van, with Harrow's disciples in pursuit.{{Ref|MK101}} ====Heading into London==== [[File:ArthurHarrowOnTheBus.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow pursues [[Steven Grant]] in [[London]]]] {{Quote|It's maddening, isn't it? The [[Khonshu|voice]] in your head. Relentless, forever unsatisfied. No matter how hard you try to please, it devours you until there's nothing left but a hollow shell.|Arthur Harrow to [[Steven Grant]]|The Goldfish Problem}} After learning that his [[Disciples of Ammit|followers]] had been defeated, Harrow went to [[London]], [[England]], to find [[Steven Grant]] and followed him onto a bus. When Grant got off the bus, Harrow watched and then sent his disciples to the [[National Art Gallery]], Grant's place of work. [[File:Arthur Harrow (Museum).png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow greets [[Steven Grant]] at the [[National Art Gallery|Gallery]]]] He then made his own way to the museum and approached Grant there himself. Grant attempted to ask a security guard, [[Ronnie (Disciples of Ammit)|Ronnie]], to escort Harrow out of the museum, but Ronnie revealed to Grant that he was secretly one of Harrow's disciples. [[File:Arthur Harrow (National Art Gallery).png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow explaining [[Ammit]] to [[Steven Grant]]]] Harrow explained to Grant that the [[Egypt|Egyptian]] goddess, [[Ammit]], owned the [[Scarab of Ammit|scarab]] that his [[Moon Knight|alter]] had [[Theft of the Scarab of Ammit|stolen]] and talked about her ability to see the morality of people's past, present, and future actions. Harrow explained that Ammit could have prevented historical atrocities had her fellow gods and her own Avatar not betrayed her. [[File:Moon Knight Big Game TV Spot (3).png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow explains [[Steven Grant]]'s inner chaos]] Harrow placed his cane in Grant's hand and judged him, realizing that there was chaos within him. Grant ran away, and Harrow instructed his disciples not to follow him. [[File:AHarrowWatchesSGrantRunAway.jpeg|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow watches [[Steven Grant]] run away]] That night, Harrow had a [[Jackals|jackal]] appear in the [[National Art Gallery]], attracting [[Steven Grant]]'s attention. Harrow demanded that Grant hand over the [[Scarab of Ammit|scarab]] in exchange for his life. Believing that Grant would be killed by the jackal, Harrow left the museum.{{Ref|MK101}} ====Speaking with Steven Grant==== [[File:Arthur Harrow & Bobbi Kennedy.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow looking down upon [[Steven Grant]]]] {{Quote|[[Ammit]] will light the path to good by eradicating the choice of evil, which brings us to the [[Scarab of Ammit|scarab]]. That scarab functions as a kind of compass, leading us to Ammit's [[Tomb of Alexander the Great|tomb]].|Arthur Harrow to [[Steven Grant]]|Summon the Suit}} The next day, Harrow learned that [[Steven Grant]] was alive, and sent two of his [[Disciples of Ammit|disciples]], [[Bobbi Kennedy]] and [[Billy Fitzgerald]], to bring Grant to him. He met them at a village that he had populated with his disciples. When Grant was let out of the car, Harrow looked down at him and told Kennedy to remove his handcuffs. [[File:Arthur Harrow & Steven Grant.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow walks and speaks with [[Steven Grant]]]] Harrow then led Grant through the village, greeting people, such as [[Jamila]], as they passed, and explaining to Grant what his disciples believed. As they walked, Harrow realized that [[Khonshu]] was nearby and told Grant to ignore him. Harrow then brought Grant to a dining area and told him that [[Victor (Disciples of Ammit)|Victor]] had prepared a soup for them. [[File:Arthur Harrow (MK).png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow gets questioned by [[Steven Grant]]]] As they ate, Harrow told Grant that he used to be Khonshu's Avatar and that he had become [[Ammit]]’s as she was real justice. Grant disagreed and questioned him on whether that was fair. When he pressed him on the case of child murder, Harrow became upset and threatened Grant with his [[Staff of Ammit|cane]]. [[File:Arthur Harrow (Cult Leader).png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow asking [[Steven Grant]] for the [[Scarab of Ammit|scarab]]]] Harrow then asked Grant repeatedly about the whereabouts of the [[Scarab of Ammit|scarab]]. Grant couldn't answer, and the disciples present began to crowd closer to interrogate him. When Harrow asked one more time, [[Layla El-Faouly]] appeared and showed him that she had it. He told her she didn't understand what the importance of holding such an object was. He then asked for it, but she instead went to Grant and told him to summon the suit, before running away. [[File:Arthur summons a jackal.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow summons a [[Jackals|jackal]] from his portal]] Frustrated, Harrow pressed his cane into the ground and used Ammit's powers to open up an entry way to another [[Multiverse|dimension]]. A [[Jackals|jackal]] then crawled out from it and Harrow sent it after Grant and El-Faouly.{{Ref|MK102}} ====Receiving the Scarab ==== [[File:Arthur Harrow kills a man without judging.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow receives the [[Scarab of Ammit|scarab]] and kills a man]] Harrow made his way outside and found the [[Scarab of Ammit|scarab]] in an older man's possession. He asked the man for the scarab and then used his [[Staff of Ammit|cane]] to ruthlessly murder him. Afterwards, he looked down an alleyway and saw that he had been watched by [[Layla El-Faouly]].{{Ref|MK102}} ===Egypt Mission=== ====Finding Ammit's Tomb==== [[File:Arthur Harrow (Cairo).jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow beginning his search out in [[Cairo]]]] Harrow and his [[Disciples of Ammit|disciples]] then left [[England]] and travelled to [[Cairo]], [[Egypt]]. They trekked their way into the desert towards the Pyramids with the [[Scarab of Ammit|scarab]] pointing them in the direction to go. As they were walking, [[Bobbi Kennedy]] alerted Harrow to the fact that [[Marc Spector]] was also in Cairo. [[File:Harrow witnesses the eclipse.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow sees [[Khonshu]] sending his signal]] As they continued to work, Harrow saw the eclipse in the sky and understood it as [[Khonshu]]’s signal to the [[Ennead]], musing that Khonshu was growing desperate. He then told his disciples that he was going to be put on trial soon.{{Ref|MK103}} ====[[Trial of Arthur Harrow|Trial with the Gods]] ==== [[File:Arthur Harrow on trial.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow being put on trial with the [[Ennead]]]] {{Quote|It brings me no pleasure to tell you that [[Marc Spector|this]] is a deeply troubled man. [[Khonshu]] is taking advantage of him, the same way that he abused me, the way he aspires to abuse this [[Ennead Council|court]]. Take action now.|Arthur Harrow to [[Ennead Council]]|The Friendly Type}} Shortly, Harrow was summoned by the [[Ennead Council]] into the [[Great Pyramid of Giza]] to be put on trial. [[Osiris]] told him that [[Khonshu]] had accused him of wanting to resurrect [[Ammit]], and asked if that was true. Harrow denied the accusation and stated he had simply been touring the pyramids. He then told them that Khonshu's Avatar, [[Marc Spector]], was a deeply unwell man who suffered from {{WPS|Dissociative Identity Disorder|dissociative identity disorder}}. When Spector, as Khonshu, attempted to punch Harrow, [[Osiris]] had then stopped him and told him they would not allow violence there. They then concluded that Harrow was innocent and dismissed the trial. Harrow looked at Spector and then left quietly.{{Ref|MK103}} ====Meeting Anton Mogart==== [[File:Arthur Harrow.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow confronts [[Marc Spector]] again]] Harrow had his [[Disciples of Ammit|disciples]] keep watch on [[Marc Spector]] and was notified by some that Spector and [[Layla El-Faouly]] went to find [[Senfu]]’s sarcophagus. Harrow then made his way to their location at [[Anton Mogart]]’s estate and attempted to turn Mogart against Spector and El-Faouly. [[File:Arthur Harrow & Anton Mogart.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow shows the [[Staff of Ammit|Staff]] to [[Anton Mogart]]]] He then told El-Faouly that Spector was hiding something from her. He showed Mogart his [[Staff of Ammit|cane]] and used [[Ammit]]’s powers to destroy the sarcophagus. He then made his exit as a fight broke out.{{Ref|MK103}} ====Speaking to Khonshu==== [[File:Harrow looks at the sky.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow sees the sky]] {{Quote|Had you not broken me so completely, I might never have known the value of healing. I'm going to do what you could not. And when it's finished, I want you to remember one thing. Your torment forged me. I owe my victory to you.|Arthur Harrow to [[Khonshu]]|The Friendly Type}} On his way out of the estate, Harrow saw the sky dramatically change. The stars moved around as if the sky was changing to a new night. Knowing this was [[Khonshu]]'s doing, Harrow visited the pyramid as he figured Khonshu would get imprisoned for his acts. Harrow saw [[Khonshu's Ushabti]] and talked to it. He admitted the Khonshu that he had a secret. He liked dealing pain and death on behalf of Khonshu. He then told him that he owed his victory to Khonshu as he was the one that broke Harrow, allowing him to feel the value of healing.{{Ref|MK103}} ====Confronting Layla El-Faouly==== [[File:The Tomb (51).png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow speaks to [[Layla El-Faouly]]]] {{Quote|Your [[Abdallah El-Faouly|father]] was [[Raid of Khonshu's Temple|murdered]] by mercenaries. No one knows who they were, do they?"
"You're saying that [[Marc Spector|Marc]] was one of them?|Arthur Harrow and [[Layla El-Faouly]]|The Tomb}} The group found the tomb holding [[Ammit's Ushabti]]. Harrow entered and walked around until he hit a room that had a giant cliff. He watched from a distance as [[Layla El-Faouly]] battled a [[Heka Priests|Heka Priest]]. She was eventually able to defeat it, throwing the priest over the ledge. Harrow spoke up, saying she handled it beautifully. [[File:Arthur Harrow (The Tomb).jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow manipulates Layla]] El-Faouly called Harrow condescending and walked away. However, Harrow caught her attention by calling her a little scarab, just as her [[Abdallah El-Faouly|father]] called her. When she walked back, Harrow explained to her his purpose and how [[Ammit]] revealed the sins to him. Harrow then sat down, right on the edge as if he was oblivious to the danger. He talked about how [[Marc Spector]] was in agony. He further explained that Spector was in agony because of a secret. Harrow alluded to Spector's involvement in Abdallah's death.{{Ref|MK104}} ====Killing Marc Spector==== [[File:Arthur Harrow (Ammit's Tomb).png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow finds the tomb]] {{Quote|I can't save anyone who won't save themselves.|Arthur Harrow|The Tomb}} Harrow was then able to find the tomb of [[Alexander the Great]] and saw [[Marc Spector]] there. Spector took an ax, holding it high. Harrow talked to him about the first day he felt that [[Khonshu]] was gone and the freeing feeling he felt. He related this to Spector now that Khonshu was gone. Since Spector was now free, Harrow gave him a choice. [[File:Arthur Harrow shoots Marc Spector.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow shoots Spector]] Spector did not choose what Harrow liked and slashed at Harrow's men with his ax, throwing it at the man in front of Harrow. Harrow then took a gun and shot Spector, who looked at Harrow in shock. Harrow then shot again, the second bullet to the chest. Spector collapsed into the water below, dying.{{Ref|MK104}} Harrow then talked to the corpse of Spector as he was lifted from the water. He placed the [[Scarab of Ammit]] on Spector's chest, referring to him as Spector, Grant, and whoever else was in there.{{Ref|MK106}} ===Unleashing Ammit=== ====Judging Guards==== [[File:Arthur Harrow vs. Egyptian Patrol Agency.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow judges the [[Egyptian Patrol Agency]] officers]] Now in possession of [[Ammit's Ushabti]], Harrow the disciples drove away. While driving on the road, they were stopped by officers from the [[Egyptian Patrol Agency]]. Harrow stepped out of the car, causing a confrontation. Harrow then proceeded to stomp the [[Staff of Ammit]] onto the ground, which checked all of the officers' souls. Every officer but one fell to the ground dead. Harrow congratulated the one officer and thanked him for being a good person.{{Ref|MK106}} ====Ammit's Avatar==== [[File:Arthur Harrow and Disciples.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow speaks to [[Ammit]]]] The disciples got to the [[Great Pyramid of Giza]] and walked up a fair distance. Harrow then used the [[Staff of Ammit]] to open the gateway. Arriving inside, Harrow immediately overwhelmed and killed [[Selim]], [[Yatzil]], [[Horus' Avatar]], [[Tefnut's Avatar]], and [[Isis' Avatar]]. He then smashed [[Ammit's Ushabti]] onto the ground, releasing [[Ammit|her]]. Ammit asked who released her and Harrow humbly said it was him. She then judged him, saying he was broken. Harrow understood, saying that he hoped his crusade would change that, but was okay with being killed. [[File:Purple Eyes (Arthur Harrow).png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow possessed by Ammit]] Ammit then said that since he freed her, she would spare him. She then commanded Harrow to be her Avatar, which he graciously accepted. Suddenly, [[Khonshu]] arrived. Harrow told his disciples to find whoever freed him. Khonshu and Ammit talked while Harrow warned her not to give in to Khonshu's words. Khonshu and Ammit fought until Khonshu finally left.{{Ref|MK106}} ====Fighting Moon Knight==== [[File:Harrow over Cairo.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow on the top of the [[Great Pyramid of Giza]]]] Everyone went outside to cleanse [[Cairo]] of their souls. Harrow went to the top of the [[Great Pyramid of Giza]] and stomped the ground, letting out a wave of energy to people's souls. [[Moon Knight]] then arrived, being healed by [[Khonshu]]. Harrow ran at Moon Knight and jumped, clashing with him. Harrow held onto Spector as he flew into the sky. Suddenly, Spector changed to [[Mr. Knight]], which did not have a cape, allowing him to fall. When Spector returned, Harrow grabbed onto him once again. crashing into the street. [[File:Arthur Harrow vs. Moon Knight.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow plunges the [[Staff of Ammit]] into Spector's chest]] Harrow and Moon Knight fought on the street. Harrow gained the upper hand and was about to blast Spector when he was suddenly tackled by the [[Scarlet Scarab]]. Now in a three way fight, Harrow did his best, fighting very well. He then blasted a van full of people, distracting Scarlet Scarab. Moon Knight got Harrow to stop by tackling him. The fight went on until Harrow got the upperhand again, deflecting the [[Crescent Darts]] into El-Faouly. He plunged the [[Staff of Ammit]] into Spector's chest, nearly killing him. Suddenly, Spector blacked out. When he got up, he seemed to be a different person, more violent. He crusaded through everyone, not being able to be defeated. Harrow was then taken down by Moon Knight, who changed into a hidden altar, [[Jake Lockley]]. Harrow was near dead as Lockley was about to chop Harrow's skull with his own staff. Suddenly, Lockley changed back into Spector, leaving Harrow on the ground.{{Ref|MK106}} ====Trapping Ammit==== [[File:Moon Knight and Scarlet Scarab trap Ammit in Arthur Harrow's body.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow transforms into [[Ammit]]'s cell]] The injured and unconscious form of Harrow was taken into the [[Great Pyramid of Giza]], and placed upon an alter. [[Scarlet Scarab]] and [[Moon Knight]] then started a chant, creating a purple band around Harrow. This chant was a spell that forced [[Ammit]] into Harrow's body, which would make her mortal. When the spell was done, Harrow was bound with Ammit and in front of Moon Knight. [[Khonshu]] ordered Spector to kill Harrow, but Spector refused, instead making Khonshu free Spector from Moon Knight.{{Ref|MK106}} ===Capture and Demise=== [[File:Arthur Harrow (Sienkiewicz Psychiatric Hospital).jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow at [[Sienkiewicz Psychiatric Hospital]]]] {{Quote|[[Khonshu]]. You can't hurt [[Ammit|us]]|Arthur Harrow to [[Khonshu]]|Gods and Monsters}} Harrow soon ended up as a patient in the [[Sienkiewicz Psychiatric Hospital]], seated in a wheelchair. Under the influence of a sedative, he attempted to reach for a drink, which he thought was sand, only to clumsily knock it over due to the sedatives effects. A nurse noticed his difficulty and approached, but was stopped by a [[Jake Lockley|man]], who instead took Harrow's wheelchair away with a reassuring pat. [[File:Jake Finds Harrow.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow is taken by [[Jake Lockley]]]] The man wheeled a dazed Harrow through the hospital, and Harrow grew increasingly concerned as they passed dead bodies in the hall. Harrow was wheeled outside, and roughly loaded into the back of a white limousine outside the hospital. Seated inside, Harrow noticed his old master and foe, [[Khonshu]], sat opposite in a white suit. Harrow remarked that Khonshu could not hurt them, referring both to himself and the trapped [[Ammit]] within him. [[File:Harrow Demise.png|{{AltFloat}}|Harrow asks for mercy]] Khonshu instead spoke to Harrow about [[Marc Spector]], and how he was the only Avatar he would ever need. Tapping the glass, Khonshu had Harrow's kidnapper revealed to him, an alter of Spector he had not yet met, [[Jake Lockley]]. Lockley wasted no time in aiming his gun at Harrow, who panicked and tried to protest. Lockley shot Harrow four times, killing him and by extension killing Ammit, ensuring neither would be a threat again, and fulfilling Khonshu's justice. The limousine then drove away, taking Harrow's body with it.{{Ref|MK106}} ==Powers and Abilities== ===Former Powers=== {{Quote|I was [[Khonshu|his]] former Avatar. Before you. I was the Fist of Vengeance.|Arthur Harrow to [[Steven Grant]]|Summon the Suit}} *'''Divine Empowerment''': As the former Avatar of [[Khonshu]], Harrow was able to achieve many superhuman feats during his time as Moon Knight. He regained many of these abilities upon becoming the Avatar of [[Ammit]], as well as gaining several new ones. **'''Superhuman Strength''': [[File:Harrow sends Moon Knight flying in the air.png|thumb|250px|Harrow sends [[Moon Knight]] flying in the air]] Harrow possessed superhuman strength that far exceeded an ordinary human. He was able to fight against Moon Knight and Scarlet Scarab with his superhuman strength, holding his own against two other Avatars, even pulling off attacks with ease. **'''Superhuman Durability''': Thanks to his blessings from Ammit, Harrow was able to shrug off hits from Moon Knight and Scarlet Scarab, who similarly both have superhuman strength. A more impressive showing of Harrow’s durability was when he fell from the sky and crashed on the ground, with no injury whatsoever. His durability does have its limits though, as Spector’s alter ego Jake Lockley was able to nearly kill him. **'''Superhuman Speed''': ''Harrow was able to run down the [[Great Pyramid of Giza]] at superhuman levels, while simultaneously charging at Moon Knight.'' **'''Superhuman Agility''': ''Harrow was shown to jump up into the air at great heights, as seen when he was battling Moon Knight for the final time.'' **'''Superhuman Stamina''': ''Harrow could withstand fighting two Avatars at once for a long time, not displaying any signs of fatigue or needing to take a break.'' **'''Superhuman Reflexes''': Harrow was shown to dodge attacks from both Moon Knight and Scarlet Scarab. A notable feat of his inhuman reflexes is when Moon Knight grappled towards him and he dodged the hook from stabbing him with ease. **'''Regenerative Healing Factor''': Harrow was able to heal from any external wounds he received, despite the fact he did not have a [[Moon Knight's Suit|healing armor]] like Spector does. Harrow was able to recover from his injuries by Jake Lockley after an unknown period of time, though it was probably only a few weeks. Though his healing factor has its limits, as shown when he was shot dead in a limousine by Jake Lockley. **'''Life-Force Absorption/Extraction''': Even before he became Ammit's Avatar, Harrow had the ability to extract the life-force from people, and used it on people deemed bad by the [[Staff of Ammit]]. He used it on an old woman in a town full of his followers, and later on a homeless man who had picked up the Scarab. Although when he tried to use it on Steven Grant it failed to work properly on Steven because having multiple people in one body confuses the ritual. His right arm is shown to bear a strange tattoo of the [[Scales of Justice]] that shifts underneath his skin when he measures the purity of other people's souls, turning green when they are deemed "balanced", and red when they are not. **'''Power Augmentation''': With the aid of Ammit, Harrow was able to increase his own power to grant the Disciples of Ammit the ability to judge people and drain their life-force while not physically present there. {{Quote|I judge you in [[Ammit]]'s name with but a fraction of her power.|Arthur Harrow|The Goldfish Problem}} *'''[[Staff of Ammit]] Capabilities''': When ''Harrow came into possession of the Staff of Ammit, it gifted him the ability to judge the souls of individuals and deem them balanced or unbalanced. Along with that, the Staff also gave him other abilities.'' **'''Judgement Inducement''': The Staff of Ammit serves as an extension of Ammit's will, inflicting judgement on those it is placed on. The Staff can determine whether a person will be evil by peering into their past and future. Should they be deemed guilty, the Staff will take their life force. Harrow sees this judgement as absolute. ***'''Life-Force Extraction''':[[File:Arthur Harrow vs. Egyptian Patrol Agency.jpg|thumb|250px|Harrow extracting life-force]] Harrow can also use the Staff to drain the life-force from other people. Harrow used this when judges people. The staff is able to absorb multiple souls at once. **'''Conjuration''': [[File:Arthur Harrow summons a jackal.png|thumb|250px|Harrow summoning a jackal]] Harrow can use the Staff of Ammit to open a portal of purple energy to another dimension and summons [[Jackals]] to [[Earth]]. He used this ability once to summon Jackals to attack [[Moon Knight|Steven Grant]] in the National Art Gallery and later again in his base in London to retrieve the Scarab of Ammit. **'''Energy Manipulation''': Harrow was also able to use the Staff of Ammit to emit energy and direct them towards objects or opponents. ***'''Energy Blast''': The staff can fire purple energy blasts from its head. These blasts can quickly destroy a sarcophagus in the collection of Anton Mogart as well as hold back [[Moon Knight]] during their final confrontation in Cairo. When slammed into the ground, it can release a pulse of energy capable of knocking back a bunch of opponents. ** '''Power Bestowal''': When Harrow was on the pyramid, he conducted a ritual spell to unleash Ammit's full power and grant the [[Disciples of Ammit]] the ability to judge people and drain the life force out of potential sinners. His followers could then hold the hands of the citizens of Cairo and judge them. ===Abilities=== * '''Master Combatant''': [[File:Moon Knight and Arthur Harrow.png|thumb|250px|Harrow fighting against [[Moon Knight]]]] Harrow was a master combatant, being able to hold his own against both [[Moon Knight]] and [[Scarlet Scarab]]. *'''Staff Mastery''': Harrow was extremely skilled in wielding the Staff of Ammit, using it in battle against Moon Knight and Layla. *'''Axe Mastery''': Harrow is also capable of transforming the Staff of Ammit into a battle axe for melee purposes. *'''Master Tactician''': As a cult leader, Harrow can command multiple of his Disciples into following and worshiping him. *'''Marksman''': Harrow precisely shot [[Marc Spector]] twice in the torso with a revolver. * '''Multilingualism''': Harrow is fluent in his native English and can speak Ancient Egyptian, Coptic, and Arabic, as well as possessing a very rudimentary knowledge of Mandarin. ==Equipment== ===Weapons === {{Quote|It contains in it a tiny sliver of [[Ammit|her]] power.|Arthur Harrow to [[Steven Grant]]|Summon the Suit}} *'''[[Staff of Ammit]]''': Harrow uses the Staff of Ammit to judge people's morality. He places the staff in the hands of a person and holds their hand as the judgement occurs. The staff then weighs their morality and communicates to a tattoo on Harrow's arm, which changes color depending on the person's morality. If the tattoo turns green, they are deemed good, but are deemed bad if the tattoo turns red. However, it will not change color if the person's morality cannot be determined, such as [[Steven Grant]]. Harrow is also capable of transforming the staff into a battle axe for melee purposes. *'''{{WPS|Webley .455 Mk VI}}''': Harrow used a revolver to shoot [[Marc Spector]] twice in the chest. ===Other Equipment === {{Expand}} *'''[[Scarab of Ammit]]''': Harrow used the Scarab of Ammit to locate Ammit's tomb. ==Facilities== *'''[[Disciples of Ammit's London Base]]''': Harrow had a base in [[London]] for his [[Disciples of Ammit]] to use. Harrow had [[Steven Grant]] brought to the facility, where he had food with Grant and asked him for the [[Scarab of Ammit]]. [[Layla El-Faouly]] then appeared with it, so Harrow had Grant and El-Faouly attacked, but they escaped. However, Harrow retrieved the scarab and went to [[Egypt]]. *'''[[Tomb of Alexander the Great]]''': Harrow excavated the tomb of [[Alexander the Great]] and began searching for [[Ammit]] with his soldiers. He found [[Layla El-Faouly]] within the tomb and confronted her, then found [[Marc Spector]] with Ammit's ushabti, leading to a confrontation in which Harrow shot Spector multiple times. *'''[[Sienkiewicz Psychiatric Hospital]]''': After [[Ammit]] was imprisoned inside his body, Harrow was sent to this psychiatric hospital for treatment. He would later be removed from this facility, only to be shortly assassinated after, by [[Jake Lockley]]. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Allies=== *[[Ammit]] † - Idol *[[Disciples of Ammit]] - Subordinates **[[Ronnie (Disciples of Ammit)|Ronnie]] **[[Bobbi Kennedy]] **[[Billy Fitzgerald]] † **[[Jamila]] **[[Chase]] **[[Victor (Disciples of Ammit)|Victor]] **[[Alpha (Disciples of Ammit)|Alpha]] † **[[Beta (Disciples of Ammit)|Beta]] † **[[Young Punk]] † *[[Jackals]] *[[Anton Mogart]] *[[Bek]] {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Khonshu]] - Former Idol *[[Moon Knight|Marc Spector/Moon Knight]] - Successor, Victim and Attempted Killer **[[Mr. Knight|Steven Grant/Mr. Knight]] - Successor and Victim **[[Jake Lockley]] - Victim and Killer *[[Scarlet Scarab|Layla El-Faouly/Scarlet Scarab]] *[[Heka Priests]] *[[Ennead Council]] **[[Osiris]] **[[Horus]] **[[Tefnut]] **[[Isis]] **[[Hathor]] *[[Egyptian Patrol Agency]] *[[Selim]] † - Former Ally and Victim *[[Yatzil]] † - Victim *[[Horus' Avatar]] † - Victim *[[Tefnut's Avatar]] † - Victim *[[Isis' Avatar]] † - Victim {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== In chronological order: *{{Appearance|MK101}} *{{Appearance|MK102}} *{{Appearance|MK103}} *{{Appearance|MK104}} *{{Appearance|MK105}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|MK106}} ==Trivia== *In the comics, Arthur Harrow was a Nobel prize nominated scientist working for [[w:c:marvel:O.M.N.I.U.M. (Earth-616)|O.M.N.I.U.M.]] who was suffering from {{WPS|trigeminal neuralgia}} and whose engagement in illegal human experimentation led to conflict with [[Moon Knight]]. **The [[Marvel Cinematic Universe]] version of Harrow is similar to the '''Sun King''', a psychotic cultist and Avatar of the sun god [[Ra|Amon Ra]], who founded a community for the renegades of the society. **Harrow also borrows from the character of '''Dr. Emmet''', a female psychiatrist who ran a hospital for the mentally ill where Marc Spector was housed alongside his friends, subjecting him to torture methods such as constant sedation and shock therapy. She was also an Avatar for the goddess Ammit. *Harrow puts broken glass in his shoes is a sign of his commitment to his righteous cause and his fundamental belief in the spirit beyond the body. This shows a reason for the Staff of Ammit besides for judgment as he seemingly requires it to walk due to instability. Harrow is still capable of walking short distances without the Staff though, as shown when he was summoned before the Ennead. *Harrow claims to be a vegan. == Behind the Scenes== *[[Ethan Hawke]]'s portrayal of Arthur Harrow in ''[[Moon Knight (TV series)|Moon Knight]]'' was inspired by {{WPS|David Koresh}},[https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-news/ethan-hawke-basing-marvel-moon-knight-character-on-david-koresh-1234997400/ Ethan Hawke Is Basing His ‘Moon Knight’ Character on David Koresh, Actor Says] {{WPS|Carl Jung}},[https://ew.com/tv/ethan-hawke-moon-knight-villain/ Why Moon Knight star Ethan Hawke was 'apprehensive' about doing a superhero project] {{WPS|Nurse Ratched}},[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=R1hQ4tUtgsk Moon Knight’s Ethan Hawke on Playing a Character That’s Malevolent But Completely Sane] {{WPS|Fidel Castro}}, {{WPS|Dalai Lama}}, {{WPS|Leo Tolstoy}}, {{WPS|Jimmy Swaggart}}, {{WPS|Josef Mengele}}, and is also described as "if {{WPS|Steve Jobs}} was a bad guy."[https://www.empireonline.com/movies/news/marvel-moon-knight-brutal-kevin-feige-not-pulling-back-exclusive/ Marvel’s Moon Knight Is ‘Brutal’, Says Kevin Feige: ‘We’re Not Pulling Back’ – Exclusive Image] *According to [[Jeremy Slater]], Harrow was chosen as the main antagonist, instead of [[Raul Bushman]], citing that Bushman's motivations and character arc were too similar to [[Erik Killmonger]].[https://www.looper.com/815565/moon-knight-writer-reveals-the-surprising-reason-why-arthur-harrow-is-the-shows-villain/ Moon Knight Writer Reveals The Surprising Reason Why Arthur Harrow Is The Show's Villain] Additionally, Slater also considered [[w:c:marvel:Scarlet Fasinera (Earth-616)|Stained Glass Scarlet]] and [[w:c:marvel:Zodiac (Dark Reign) (Earth-616)|Zodiac]] as potential villains. He ultimately invented a new villain and pulled the name Arthur Harrow from a list of Moon Knight antagonists because he liked the way it sounded.[https://screenrant.com/moon-knight-villain-arthur-harrow-name-origin-explained/ Moon Knight Producer Reveals Bizarre Way Marvel Picked Villain’s Name] *[[József Kovalik Jr.]] and [[Thibault Chiron]] were stunt doubles for [[Ethan Hawke]] in the role of Arthur Harrow. *[[Simu Liu]] has criticized Harrow's lack of proficiency in his Mandarin.[https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/moon-knight-shang-chi-star-simu-liu-roasts-ethan-hawkes-mandarin-skills/ Moon Knight: Shang-Chi Star Simu Liu Roasts Ethan Hawke's Mandarin Skills] ==References== {{Gallery|Arthur Harrow/Gallery}} {{Wq}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} *{{Marvel|Sun King (Patient 86) (Earth-616)|Sun King}} [[es:Arthur Harrow]] [[pt-br:Arthur Harrow]] [[ru:Артур Хэрроу]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Moon Knight (TV series) Characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Brown Hair]] [[Category:Blue Eyes]] [[Category:Disciples of Ammit Leaders]] [[Category:Enhanced Individuals]] [[Category:Avatars]] [[Category:Moon Knights]] [[Category:Multilingual Characters]] [[Category:High Body Count]] [[Category:Deceased Characters]] [[Category:Characters Killed by Moon Knight]]
